changes.reason: Revert the failed squeeze fast-realization cap from 5.5 back to the prior convexity-friendly grid value. The latest squeeze compression cut net profit, worsened drawdown, and let portfolio worst MAE degrade to -638.75; the profitable squeeze family used higher upside capture.
Restore the fixed squeeze target from 3.25 to 4.5. Prior evidence shows the squeeze edge is clean but convex; lowering the target underpaid continuation without improving risk-adjusted behavior.

Move slope confirmation back to 0.06, tying the higher target to stronger continuation quality. This is the prior successful grid value and avoids expanding squeeze entries while recovering upside capture.

recommendation.expected_effect: Should recover the run_029/run_030 squeeze convexity profile: higher net profit, better growth quality, and restored portfolio MAE band versus the latest failed compression. Tradeoff is slightly fewer or longer squeeze trades, so reject if squeeze PnL/exposure drops, net profit fails to retain 98% of run_029, or worst MAE remains worse than -527.
recommendation.label: squeeze-convexity-reversion
recommendation.rationale: Use exactly one legal family: batch_b_squeeze_long_take_profit. The latest run is worse specifically because the squeeze-fast-realization experiment lowered targets and tightened slope; experiment memory shows that same keyset hurt score by -127.9, net profit by -93.0, drawdown by +0.001, and worst MAE by -111.75. The best next move is not a new global filter or 1DTE tightening, but a controlled reversion to the previously successful squeeze upside-capture settings while preserving option-aware routing and all existing fallback policy.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: squeeze_long remains the cleanest VALID_0DTE profile by efficiency in the latest run: 5 trades, $477.7 PnL, 424.9 PnL/exposure hour, no slow >=12h trades, and worst MAE only -113.9. Prior squeeze upside capture with max 8, TP 4.5, and slope 0.06 materially improved from a damaged base, while the latest compression to max 5.5, TP 3.25, and slope 0.05 hurt objective and net profit.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Squeeze Convexity Reversion
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Small sample; restoring larger targets can lengthen holds or fail to reproduce prior winners. Promotion requires portfolio-level score and net profit recovery, not just cleaner squeeze attribution.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE squeeze_long entries with confirmed option context and strong ES VWAP continuation slope.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Total risk-adjusted score improves versus run_033 and approaches run_029, net profit remains >=98% of run_029, squeeze_long PnL/exposure stays strong, slow_12h remains 0, and total worst MAE is no worse than -527.
